Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The PM4Py loop semantics here are execute the do child E then either exit or execute the redo child P and return to E This produces traces such as E E P E E P E P E unbounded repetition

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Two distinct deviations from the ideal flow follow Approval is optional Since the loop can exit immediately after the first E a claim can reach C close with no P approve event at all Approval and evaluation can repeat indefinitely and every trace ends with E meaning the last recorded act is always an evaluation never an approval

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Customer notification is entirely optional Silently skipping N means a claim can be approved denied and closed without the customer ever being informed

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The partial order contains only R A A loop loop xor A C C is ordered only with respect to A Consequently in a StrictPartialOrder C is concurrent with loop and with xor Valid interleavings include R A C E N R A E C R A E P C E N This breaks the fundamental invariant that C is the terminal event of the case

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: There is no activity in the model that corresponds to rejection denial the only decision node is P approve Real data may contain a Reject Claim activity that the model cannot reproduce The model does not constrain resource to be the assigned adjuster nor the adjuster specialization to match claims claim type This permits E P performed by an unqualified or unassigned resource a segregation of duties gap

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: H1 Partially implemented business rule fast track auto close for low value claims A rule such as claims under 500 may be closed without full evaluation was introduced The modeller implemented the shortcut A C optional P optional N but never added the guard condition amount threshold claim type

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: H2 Discovery artifact over generalization The model was mined from a log rather than hand designed A handful of noisy or incomplete cases missing E duplicated P out of order C forced the discovery algorithm to insert a loop an XOR skip and to drop the xor C edge to keep fitness at 1 0

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: H3 Miscommunication between departments Claims Intake and Customer Communications each assumed the other owned notification so N became optional Similarly the Closing team defines closed as file archived while Evaluation defines it as decision made producing overlapping C and E

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: H4 Technical defect in the workflow engine A missing precondition check or an asynchronous queued close job or a timer based auto close let C fire while evaluation was still open Retry logic or a double click on Approve produced duplicate P events

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: H5 Modelling tool methodology weakness The modeller built a StrictPartialOrder by hand and simply forgot the xor C edge the tool performed no soundness terminality check and issued no warning that C was unordered with respect to loop

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: H6 Genuine rework loop from poor first pass quality The E P loop reflects reality incomplete documentation forces re evaluation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Variant trace frequency baseline for H2 Interpretation if conformant traces dominate and deviations are a long tail of singletons H2 noise over fitting If a deviating variant is a large clean block H1 H3 real institutionalized shortcut

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Anomaly A1 Closed without evaluation or without approval Closed with no approval at all Closed with no evaluation at all Aggregate is the shortcut amount driven tests H1 A monotone decline of no approval as amount rises strongly supports H1

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Anomaly A2 Multiple approvals rework loop Claims approved more than once with loop depth Distribution of loop iterations Duplicate P events close together by the same resource system defect H4 Sub minute duplicates by one resource H4 double submit retry Multi day gaps with different resources and additional info reasons H6 genuine rework

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Anomaly B Notification skipped Overall skip rate Skip rate by claim type and by region adjuster team tests H3 Notification AFTER closing ordering violation of a different kind A skip rate concentrated in one region or one team H3 Uniform around 50 percent across all segments the XOR is truly unguarded H5 H1

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Anomaly C Premature non terminal close C is not the last event of the case C before evaluation before approval before notification Who or what performs the premature closes tests H4 Temporal clustering did the defect start on a release date H4 H1 A step change at one week plus SYSTEM or BATCH resource H4 Zero rows anywhere H5 model only defect

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Anomaly D Resource competence segregation of duties checks Evaluation or approval by someone who is not the assigned adjuster Specialization mismatch Same person evaluates and approves Activities present in the log but absent from the model

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Formal conformance cross check High fitness and low precision the model allows far more than reality does anomalies are modelling artifacts H5 tighten the model High fitness and high precision the anomalous paths are genuinely executed H1 H3 H4 H6 fix the process not just the model

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Recommended Corrected Model Exactly one approval with bounded explicit rework Explicit decision approve OR reject no silent skip of the decision Notification mandatory restores C as the terminal event Plus outside the control flow a data guard for any fast track path a four eyes rule on E P a specialization equals claim type assignment rule and a workflow engine precondition that blocks C unless a decision and notification exist
